summary.en: Salem’s historic and commercial heart, a neighborhood of contrasts where magnificent Federal-style mansions stand blocks away from kitschy statues and modern decay.

name.en: The Waterfront: Pickering Wharf & Derby Street

summary.en: This is where Salem's maritime soul resides, with the smell of salt in the air and the sound of rigging clinking against masts. It's a blend of a working waterfront and a polished tourist destination.
The city’s nostalgic, communal backyard, this area offers an old-fashioned amusement park feel, coastal views, and a more rugged park experience away from the downtown crowds.

description.en: A beloved local institution with a cult-like following, serving warm, gooey cookies and soft-serve ice cream on weekends with a secret, rotating menu.
A masterwork by renowned architect Samuel McIntire, this hall is a testament to the immense wealth generated during Salem's 'Age of Sail'.
A controversial bronze statue of Elizabeth Montgomery that embodies Salem’s ongoing identity crisis between its real history and pop culture fame.
A case study in urban decay and perseverance, where small businesses operate within a privately-owned mall showing significant signs of neglect.
A stunningly preserved avenue of grand, Federal-style mansions that feels a world away from the witch tourism just blocks away.
A reliable spot for excellent coffee and tea with a harbor view, consistently pointed to by locals as the real deal among more tourist-focused businesses.
A full-scale replica of a 1797 East Indiaman merchant ship that serves as a powerful visual reminder of the global trade that built the city.
A living piece of history, this replica of a famously fast War of 1812 privateer takes customers for cruises around the harbor.
The nation's first National Historic Site, preserving the wharves, warehouses, and Custom House from Salem's 'Age of Sail'.
The grand building where Nathaniel Hawthorne worked and was inspired to write 'The Scarlet Letter', a centerpiece of the historic waterfront.
A lively wharf area that blends tourist shops and restaurants with the authentic atmosphere of a working marina and harbor.
A multi-generational North Shore legend whose signature scent of fresh popcorn defines the atmosphere of Salem Willows.
A time warp of Skee-Ball, vintage pinball, and classic ticket-redemption games offering a dose of pure, uncomplicated fun.
A peaceful spot offering a unique vantage point with panoramic views of the coastline from Beverly to Marblehead.
A former Coast Guard station offering a rugged coastal experience with old fortifications, a historic lighthouse, and sweeping views of Salem Sound.
A historic lighthouse situated on a rocky shoreline within Winter Island Park, offering a dramatic coastal scene.
Named for the graceful white willow trees planted in 1801, this public park is the city's charming, old-fashioned communal backyard.

prompt.en: Find a spot where a historic building stands next to a modern one. Take a photo that captures the contrast between the two eras.
Get a close-up shot of an architectural detail on a Federal-style mansion, like an ornate doorway, a fanlight window, or an iron fence.
Photograph a witch-themed object or sign that feels amusingly out of place against the backdrop of serious colonial history.
Find any historical marker or plaque on a building or sidewalk and take a selfie with it, looking studious.
Discover and photograph the storefront of a local, independent business that has nothing to do with witches or tourism.
Find a detail that tells a story of urban decay—a cracked brick, a faded sign, a neglected corner—and capture it in a poignant photo.
Take a photo of a historic building's reflection in a modern window or a puddle.
Grab a coffee or snack from a local spot and take an artistic photo of it with the street scene in the background.
Play with light and shadow. Capture a photo where the long shadows of historic buildings create interesting patterns on the street or sidewalk.
Find a narrow alley or backstreet and take a photo looking down it, capturing its unique texture and atmosphere.
Against a brick or colonial-style backdrop, take a selfie with a very stern, serious, 17th-century Puritan expression.
Search for a building with its construction date carved or mounted on it. Take a photo of the date.
Find a thick rope used to tie up a boat and take a detailed, close-up photo of the rope, the cleat, or the knot.
Take a selfie with the masts of the tall ships in the background. Bonus points for a windswept look!
Capture a photo of a boat, building, or the sky reflected in the harbor water.
Photograph a flag (any flag!) flying in the wind against the sky or water.
Find a pier, piling, or bench with weathered, textured wood and take a close-up shot.
Take a picture of a seagull. Try to capture it in a unique pose—in flight, perched on a piling, or watching the world go by.
Find a boat in the marina and take a photo of its name painted on the hull.
Look up! Frame a photo of the rigging and masts of a ship, creating a complex pattern of lines against the sky.
Get a drink from a local cafe and photograph it on a table with the harbor blurred in the background.
Capture a scene that shows this is a 'working' waterfront—a lobster trap, a fishing boat, or someone working on their vessel.
Pose for a selfie while shielding your eyes with your hand, as if you're a sailor spotting land for the first time in months.
Take a photo that features both the historic red brick of a waterfront warehouse and a view of the water in the same shot.
Capture the energy of the arcade by taking a photo of the flashing lights and vibrant colors of the games.
Take a photo looking up through the branches of one of the park's namesake willow trees.
Get a classic summer treat like popcorn or ice cream and take a 'food portrait' of it with the park or arcade blurred in the background.
From the beach or a viewpoint, take a panoramic photo that captures the sweep of the coastline and the water.
In front of an arcade game, take a selfie with your most triumphant 'I just won the jackpot!' expression.
On Winter Island, take a dramatic photo of the rocky shore meeting the water.
Find a piece of the island's military past—like a concrete bunker or fortification—and photograph it.
Capture a scene that feels timeless and nostalgic—kids playing, a family on a picnic blanket, or an old-fashioned park bench.
Find an interesting object on the beach—a unique rock, a piece of sea glass, or a shell—and take a close-up photo of it.
Look across the water and take a photo of a landmark in a neighboring town, like a lighthouse or church steeple.
If you're here at the end of the day, capture a silhouette of a person, a tree, or a building against the sunset over the water.
Find a walking path or trail in one of the parks and take a photo from a low angle, making it look like an invitation to an adventure.
